title: Query Understanding, Filters, Rewriting, Expansion, and Decomposition · Program Brief: Rag Query Expansion Feedback
summary: A model rewrites every query, drops quoted identifiers and dates, invents metadata filters, and issues six hidden searches when the user needed one clarification. Predict the stakeholder decision, corpus and query identity, relevance assumption, failure cost, and evidence that would falsify the proposed RAG claim before opening the governing source. Planned artifact: Add and verify the query expansion feedback increment in bounded query-plan compiler. Learners progress through prediction, expli…
objectives: Evaluate synonyms, acronyms, spelling, pseudo-relevance feedback, and domain vocabulary without drifting away from user intent.
Preserve original query, rewrite purpose, version, generated alternatives, filters, and per-rewrite retrieval contribution.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""rag-retrieval-augmented-generation cumulative evidence-grounded retrieval portfolio.

Browser execution uses deterministic pure-Python models and fixed fixtures only.
Native ML packages, files, models, vector stores, provider APIs, networks, credentials,
OCR, load, faults, recovery systems, and production effects require authorized transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class RagEvidence:
    corpus_version: str
    document_id: str
    chunk_id: str
    query_id: str
    source_span: str


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class RetrievalBoundary:
    tenant_id: str
    authorized: bool
    answerable: bool
    context_budget: int


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Python RAG engineering portfolio ready"

content.code:
# Evidence: rag-worked-rag-query-understanding-4
# Competency: rag-worked-rag-query-understanding-4.
# Case 1655: an incident-response knowledge search.
# Operating constraint: measure retrieval and generation separately before accepting an end-to-end score.
# Changed case: double corpus size and candidate depth while preserving the latency, memory, and cost envelope.
def worked_rag_query_understanding_4(query: str = "reset MFA for contractor", intent: str = "procedure", filters_valid: bool = True, ambiguity: int = 1, rewrite_count: int = 2, original_preserved: bool = True) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_worked4 = "worked4-4dekrow-1655"
    assert evidence_variant_worked4
    evidence_axes_worked4 = (
        "corpusworked4",
        "query4dekrow",
        "tenant1655",
        "indexworked41655",
        "prompt4dekrow1655",
        "evaluation1655worked4",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_worked4)) == 6
    if not query.strip() or not intent:
        return False, 0, "query-intent"
    if not filters_valid:
        return False, rewrite_count, "filter-schema"
    if ambiguity < 0 or rewrite_count < 0 or rewrite_count > 5:
        return False, rewrite_count, "rewrite-budget"
    if not original_preserved:
        return False, rewrite_count, "query-lineage"
    if ambiguity > 2:
        return False, rewrite_count, "clarification-required"
    assert rewrite_count <= 5
    return True, rewrite_count, "bounded-query-plan"

steps.instruction:
Write the theory evidence note for Rag Query Expansion Feedback: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — A theory RAG engineering team handles case 2704 during a public-benefits policy change. Build deterministic Python evidence to classify intent, validate filters, bound rewrites, preserve lineage, and ask when ambiguity remains; measure retrieval and generation separately before accepting an end-to-end score; then change tenant, language, date, and document version and reject stale or unauthorized evidence. Browser code uses pure bounded models and fixed fixtures only. Native Python packages, embedding or generation models, vector databases, files, networks, credentials, provider APIs, OCR, load, faults, recovery systems, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Evaluate synonyms, acronyms, spelling, pseudo-relevance feedback, and domain vocabulary without drifting away from user intent.

Evidence target: RAG and Python Defend the Rag Query Expansion Feedback model · Theory Rag Query Expansion Feedback.
steps.why: Retrieval and explanation expose gaps that recognition questions can hide. For RAG and Python Defend the Rag Query Expansion Feedback model · Theory Rag Query Expansion Feedback, keep Rag Query Expansion Feedback connected to executable Python retrieval, grounding, evaluation, and evidence decision evidence.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Evaluate synonyms, acronyms, spelling, pseudo-relevance feedback, and domain vocabulary without drifting away from user intent.

hints: For RAG and Python Defend the Rag Query Expansion Feedback model · Theory Rag Query Expansion Feedback, name the stakeholder, corpus, source, document, chunk, model, index, query, relevance, tenant, context, citation, evaluation, and transfer boundary, Rag Query Expansion Feedback precondition, or invariant that must hold.

At RAG and Python Defend the Rag Query Expansion Feedback model · Theory Rag Query Expansion Feedback, trace this concrete input and predict ingestion, normalization, retrieval, filter, fusion, reranking, context, claim, citation, abstention, latency, cost, failure, and recovery evidence before reviewing it: A theory RAG engineering team handles case 2704 during a public-benefits policy change. Build deterministic Python evidence to classify intent, validate filters, bound rewrites, preserve lineage, and ask when ambiguity remains; measure retrieval and generation separately before accepting an end-to-end score; then change tenant, language, date, and document version and reject stale or unauthorized evidence. Browser code uses pure bounded models and fixed fixtures only. Native Python packages, embedding or generation models, vector databases, files, networks, credentials, provider APIs, OCR, load, faults, recovery systems, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Evaluate synonyms, acronyms, spelling, pseudo-relevance feedback, and domain vocabulary without drifting away from user intent.

For RAG and Python Defend the Rag Query Expansion Feedback model · Theory Rag Query Expansion Feedback, challenge the documented misconception with changed-query, changed-corpus, relevance, retrieval, grounding, citation, adversarial, accessibility, failure, recovery, and controlled native or production-transfer evidence: Adding more related terms cannot reduce retrieval precision.
